Description:
Please note that these are just estimates, 
			and the actual amount of each resource type 
			in any given C-type asteroid can vary greatly. 
			The composition of asteroids can change over 
			time due to collisions, fragmentation, and 
			other processes.
Here is a more detailed breakdown of each resource type:

Water ice: is the most abundant resource in C-type asteroids.
			It is thought to make up about 5-10% of the mass of these 
			asteroids. Water ice could be used for drinking water, 
			irrigation, and industrial purposes. It could also be used 
			to make rocket fuel and hydrogen peroxide.

Hydrates: minerals that contain water molecules bound 
			to their crystal structure. They are thought to make up 
			about 0.5-1% of the mass of C-type asteroids. Hydrates 
			could be a source of water and other valuable minerals.

Silicate minerals: the most common type of mineral in 
			the standard solar system. They are thought to make up about 
			40-50% of the mass of C-type asteroids. Silicate minerals 
			could be used to make cement, glass, and other materials.

Organic compounds: carbon-based molecules that can form 
			the basis of life. They are thought to make up about 5-10% 
			of the mass of C-type asteroids. Organic compounds could be 
			used to make fuels, plastics, and pharmaceuticals.

Metallic ore is a mixture of metals, such as iron, nickel, 
			and cobalt. It is thought to make up about 0.1-0.5% of the 
			mass of C-type asteroids. Metallic ore could be used to make 
			steel, aluminum, and other alloys.

Nitrates: salts that contain the nitrate ion (NO3-). They 
			are thought to make up about 0.1-0.5% of the mass of C-type 
			asteroids. Nitrates are an essential nutrient for plants, 
			and they could be used to fertilize crops or to produce biofuels.

Rare metals: metals that are not as common as iron, nickel, 
			and cobalt. They are thought to make up about 0.01-0.1% of the 
			mass of C-type asteroids. Rare metals are used in a wide variety 
			of applications, including electronics, aerospace, and medicine.

Exotic minerals: minerals that have unusual properties or 
			compositions. They are thought to make up about 0.01-0.1% of 
			the mass of C-type asteroids. Exotic minerals could be used in 
			new technologies or for scientific research.

Uraninite: a mineral that contains uranium, a radioactive 
			element. It is thought to make up about 0.001-0.01% of the 
			mass of C-type asteroids. Uraninite could be used to generate 
			electricity or to produce radioactive isotopes for medical and 
			industrial purposes.

Others: possible resources that could be found in C-type 
			asteroids, such as sulfur, iodine, and phosphorus. These 
			resources could be used in a variety of applications, 
			including agriculture, medicine, and chemical manufacturing.
